auth_notes: Requires application registration in the iManage Control Center to obtain a Client ID and Client Secret. Often requires an 'API Key' (App Key) passed in headers (X-App-Key). Supports both Authorization Code flow for user-level access and Client Credentials for service-level access.
rate_limits: Varies by cloud environment; typically throttled at 1,000 requests per minute per user, but subject to specific tenant policies.
gotchas: File uploads require multipart/form-data where metadata must be provided as a specific JSON string part named 'profile'.
API access must be explicitly whitelisted/enabled for the specific application within the iManage Control Center by a system administrator.
Document locking and checkout logic must be handled manually via API calls to prevent edit conflicts.
research_notes: The integration will rely heavily on the HTTP Request node. For OAuth 2.0, n8n's built-in OAuth2 credentials can be used, but the 'X-App-Key' must be manually added to the headers of every request. Handling file buffers (binary data) for uploads and downloads is the most time-consuming part of the implementation.

effort_recommendation.rationale: The integration will rely heavily on the HTTP Request node.
